summary: Hungarian Nobel laureate. Known for Fatelessness (about a boy's Holocaust experience), Kaddish for a Child Not Born, and Liquidation. Drew on his own Auschwitz and Buchenwald experiences.

works.name: Fatelessness
works.description: Semi-autobiographical novel about Gyuri Köves learning to survive concentration camps.

clues.clue: Gyuri Köves learns to survive Zeitz from Bandi Citrom after earlier stops in Buchenwald and Auschwitz; drawn from author's life
examples: Gyuri Koves learns how to survive Zeitz from Bandi Citrom after earlier stops in Buchenwald and Auschwitz

works.name: Kaddish for a Child Not Born
works.description: Novel about refusing to have children in a post-Holocaust world.

clues.clue: B. constantly recalls 'no' — his refusal to bring a child into a world of suffering; two uses of 'no' (wife suggests children, doctor asks)
examples: he constantly recalls the word 'no,' indicating his refusal to subject a child to the suffering of the world

clues.clue: Nobel laureate from Hungary; also wrote Detective Story and Liquidation

comprehensive_summary: Imre Kertész (1929-2016) was a Hungarian Nobel Prize-winning author who drew on his own Holocaust experiences at Auschwitz and Buchenwald. Fatelessness is a semi-autobiographical novel in which Gyuri Köves learns to survive the Zeitz camp from Bandi Citrom. Kaddish for a Child Not Born centers on B.'s refusal to bring a child into a world that allowed the Holocaust, recalled through the word 'no.' He also wrote Detective Story (about a former secret policeman) and Liquidation.

cards.front: Novel: Gyuri Köves learns to survive the Zeitz camp from Bandi Citrom after Buchenwald and Auschwitz; drawn from the author's life
Novel: B. refuses to have children in a post-Holocaust world; recalls the word 'no' — once when his wife suggests children, once when a doctor asks
cards.back: Kaddish for a Child Not Born (Imre Kertész)
